metadata.title: Important Laws on Finance & Financial Crimes

questions.question: The Aadhaar (Targeted Delivery of Financial and other Subsidies, benefits and services) Act was enacted in which year?
questions.explanation: The Aadhaar (Targeted Delivery of Financial and other Subsidies, benefits and services) Act was enacted in the year 2016. Its purpose was to provide legal backing to the Aadhaar unique identification number project.

questions.question: Which chapter details the functions and powers of the Unique Identification Authority of India constituted under Aadhaar (Targeted Delivery of Financial and other Subsidies, benefits and services) Act, 2016 ?
questions.explanation: The chapter IV details the functions and powers of the Unique Identification Authority of India constituted under Aadhaar (Targeted Delivery of Financial and other Subsidies, benefits and services) Act, 2016.

questions.question: Any fees collected and revenue generated by the UIDAI will be deposited in which of the following?
questions.explanation: Any fees collected and revenue generated by the Unique Identification Authority of India (UIDAI) will be deposited in the Consolidated Fund of India. It is stated in Chapter V: Grants, Accounts and Audit and Annual Report of the Aadhaar (Targeted Delivery of Financial and other Subsidies, benefits and services) Act, 2016.

questions.question: The Banking Regulation Act was enacted in which year ?
questions.explanation: The Banking Regulation Act was in the year 1949 by the Parliament of India. It regulates all banking firms in India. It provides a framework under which commercial banking in India is supervised and regulated.

questions.question: The Banking Regulation Act, 1949 gives which of the following the power to license banks, have regulation over shareholding and voting rights of shareholders?

options.label: C
options.text: National Bank of Agriculture and Rural Development

questions.explanation: The Banking Regulation Act, 1949 gives the Reserve Bank of India the power to license banks, have regulation over shareholding and voting rights of shareholders. It gives RBI the power to supervise the appointment of the boards and management.
